2603.13373.title: Ethical Fairness without Demographics in Human-Centered AI
2603.13373.paper_summary: Computational models are increasingly embedded in human-centered domains such as healthcare, education, workplace analytics, and digital well-being, where their predictions directly influence individual outcomes and collective welfare. In such contexts, achieving high accuracy alone is insufficient; models must also act ethically and equitably across diverse populations. However, fair AI approaches that rely on demographic attributes are impractical, as such information is often unavailable, privacy-sensitive, or restricted by regulatory frameworks. Moreover, conventional parity-based fairness approaches, while aiming for equity, can inadvertently violate core ethical principles by trading off subgroup performance or stability. To address this challenge, we present Flare (Fisher-guided LAtent-subgroup learning with do-no-harm REgularization), the first demographic-agnostic framework that aligns algorithmic fairness with ethical principles through the geometry of optimization. Flare leverages Fisher Information to regularize curvature, uncovering latent disparities in model behavior without access to demographic or sensitive attributes. By integrating representation, loss, and curvature signals, it identifies hidden performance strata and adaptively refines them through collaborative but do-no-harm optimization, enhancing each subgroup's performance while preserving global stability and ethical balance. We also introduce BHE (Beneficence-Harm Avoidance-Equity), a novel metric suite that operationalizes ethical fairness evaluation beyond statistical parity. Extensive evaluations across diverse physiological (EDA), behavioral (IHS), and clinical (OhioT1DM) datasets show that Flare consistently enhances ethical fairness compared to state-of-the-art baselines.
2603.13373.paper_summary_zh: 計算模型越來越多地嵌入以人為中心的領域，如醫療保健、教育、工作場所分析和數位福祉，在這些領域中，它們的預測直接影響個體結果和集體福祉。<lb/>在這樣的背景下，僅僅實現高準確性是不夠的；模型還必須在不同人群中以道德和公平的方式運作。<lb/>然而，依賴於人口統計特徵的公平人工智慧方法並不切實際，因為這些信息往往不可用、涉及隱私或受到監管框架的限制。<lb/>此外，傳統的基於平等的公平方法，雖然旨在實現公平，但可能會無意中違反核心道德原則，因為它們在子群體表現或穩定性之間進行權衡。<lb/>為了解決這一挑戰，我們提出了Flare（Fisher-guided LAtent-subgroup learning with do-no-harm REgularization），這是第一個與人口統計無關的框架，通過優化的幾何形狀將算法公平性與道德原則對齊。<lb/>Flare利用Fisher信息來正則化曲率，揭示模型行為中的潛在差異，而無需訪問人口統計或敏感屬性。<lb/>通過整合表示、損失和曲率信號，它識別隱藏的性能層次，並通過協作但不造成傷害的優化自適應地細化它們，增強每個子群體的性能，同時保持全球穩定性和道德平衡。<lb/>我們還介紹了BHE（Beneficence-Harm Avoidance-Equity），這是一套新穎的指標，超越統計平等，實現道德公平評估的操作化。<lb/>在多個生理（EDA）、行為（IHS）和臨床（OhioT1DM）數據集上的廣泛評估顯示，與最先進的基準相比，Flare始終提升了道德公平性。
2603.13373.author: Shaily Roy et.al.
2603.13373.authors: Shaily Roy, Harshit Sharma, Asif Salekin
